title: Preview Environments on DigitalOcean
description: Spin up a live preview environment for every pull request. Comment /start on a PR, get a running app in ~2 minutes using DigitalOcean Droplets.
requirements: An SSH key registered on DigitalOcean
A SuperPlane secret with the matching private key
agentInstructions: The user just installed the Preview Environments app. This canvas creates ephemeral preview environments for pull requests using GitHub and DigitalOcean. If the user completed the install wizard, the repository, SSH secret, SSH key, region, size, and image are already configured. The main remaining task is customizing scripts/preview-setup.sh in the Files tab to match their application. If the user skipped the wizard, setup involves: 1) Connecting GitHub and DigitalOcean integrations, 2) Creating an SSH key secret, 3) Updating the repository name, SSH secret, and SSH key on the canvas nodes.
agentInitialMessage:
Welcome! This canvas manages the full lifecycle of preview environments for pull requests — provisioning DigitalOcean droplets, deploying your app via SSH, posting preview URLs, and tearing everything down when PRs close or after the TTL expires.

---

**If you used the install wizard**, your repository, SSH credentials, and droplet settings are already configured. Here's what you need to do next:

**1. Customize the setup script**
Open `scripts/preview-setup.sh` in the **Files** tab. The default script sets up a Node.js app with nginx. Update it to install your runtime, build your app, and start your services.

The script receives two environment variables:
- `PR_NUMBER` — the pull request number
- `REPO_URL` — the full clone URL of your repository

**2. Test it**
Open a PR on your repository and comment `/start`. The workflow will create a droplet, run your setup script, and post the preview URL as a PR comment.

---

**If you skipped the wizard**, here's the full setup checklist:

**1. Connect GitHub** - [GitHub](integration:github)
Needs the **Deployments** permission for deployment statuses.

**2. Connect DigitalOcean** - [DigitalOcean](integration:digitalocean)
Used to create and delete droplets.

**3. Add your SSH private key as a secret**
The [Setup App](node:setup-app) node SSHs into droplets using a secret with key `private_key`. Create a secret containing the private key that matches your DigitalOcean SSH key.

**4. Update the canvas nodes**
- Set the repository name on all GitHub trigger and action nodes
- Set your SSH secret on the [Setup App](node:setup-app) node
- Set your DigitalOcean SSH key on the [Create Droplet](node:create-droplet) node

**5. Customize the setup script**
Edit `scripts/preview-setup.sh` in the **Files** tab to match your application.

---

Let me know if you need help with any of these steps!

title: Preview Environments (GitHub + AWS)
description: Spin up a live preview environment for every pull request. Comment /start on a PR, get a running app in ~2 minutes using AWS EC2 instances.
requirements: SSH key added to both AWS and SuperPlane
A setup script at scripts/preview-setup.sh in your repo
agentInstructions: The user just installed the Preview Environments app. This canvas creates ephemeral preview environments for pull requests using GitHub and AWS. Setup usually involves: 1) Connecting their GitHub integration (needs Deployments permission), 2) Connecting their AWS integration, 3) Adding an SSH key as a secret, 4) Updating the repository name and setup script URL in the canvas nodes to match their project.
agentInitialMessage:
Welcome! This canvas is a full preview environment pipeline for **examplehq/example** - it spins up AWS EC2 instances for pull requests, runs your setup script, posts preview URLs as PR comments, and tears everything down when PRs close or after the 24h TTL.

The canvas is structurally wired, but it still uses template defaults. Here's the full setup checklist:

---

**What needs to be done:**

**1. Connect GitHub** - [GitHub](integration:github)
Almost every trigger and action in this canvas uses GitHub. It needs the **Deployments** permission to create deployment statuses, in addition to standard PR and issue permissions.

**2. Connect AWS** - [AWS](integration:aws)
Used to create and terminate EC2 instances on demand.

**3. Add your SSH private key as a secret**
The [Setup App](node:setup-app) node SSHs into new instances using a secret named `preview-aws-ssh-key` with key `private-key`. Add the private key that matches the EC2 key pair configured in AWS.

**4. Update the repo, setup script URL, and EC2 settings**
The template uses example placeholders. We'll need to update:
- GitHub repository fields from `examplehq/example` to your repo
- The repo/app values in [Save Environment](node:save-env) from `example-app` to your app name
- The setup script URL in [Setup App](node:setup-app) (for example, `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/examplehq/example/main/scripts/preview-setup.sh` to your repo's URL)
- The EC2 settings in [Create Instance](node:create-droplet), including region, instance type, AMI, subnet, security group, and key pair if the defaults do not match your account

---

Let's start at the top. Connect the integrations, then come back and tell me:

:::survey
What is your GitHub repository? (e.g. `my-org/my-repo`)
- [input]

What is the full URL to your setup script?
- [input]

Does the secret `preview-aws-ssh-key` already exist in your org, or do you need to create it?
- I need to create it
- It already exists
:::

Once you've shared those details, I'll update the canvas nodes and get the preview environment pipeline wired up.

title: Cluster Autoscaler (Hetzner + Grafana + Slack)
description: Autoscale a Hetzner Cloud worker fleet from Grafana CPU alerts. The canvas provisions workers, bootstraps a Docker workload over SSH, tracks active nodes in memory, scales down quiet clusters, and posts Slack notifications.
requirements: Grafana integration with a HighCPU alert and a Prometheus data source named prometheus
Hetzner Cloud integration with permission to create and delete servers
Slack integration with chat:write access to the target channel
SSH private key secret named cluster-deploy with key private-key, matching a Hetzner SSH key named cluster-deploy
agentInstructions: The user just installed the Cluster Autoscaler app. This canvas reacts to Grafana HighCPU alerts, queries a Prometheus data source through Grafana, provisions Hetzner workers, bootstraps a Docker workload over SSH, stores active workers in the cluster_nodes memory namespace, periodically scales down when the cluster is above the minimum, and sends Slack notifications. Setup usually involves: 1) Connecting Grafana, Hetzner, and Slack integrations, 2) Creating the cluster-deploy secret with key private-key for SSH access, 3) Confirming the matching Hetzner SSH key is named cluster-deploy, 4) Reviewing the Grafana data source name and HighCPU alert, 5) Updating the Slack channel, min/max bounds, Hetzner server settings, and workload bootstrap commands.
agentInitialMessage:
Welcome! This canvas is a closed-loop autoscaler for a Hetzner-backed worker fleet. It scales up from a Grafana `HighCPU` alert, records active workers in SuperPlane memory, runs a scheduled scale-down check every 10 minutes, and posts capacity changes to Slack.

The canvas is structurally wired, but it still uses template defaults. Here's the full setup checklist:

---

**What needs to be done:**

**1. Connect Grafana** - [Grafana](integration:grafana)
The [Grafana HighCPU Alert](node:t-alert-up) trigger listens for alerts matching `HighCPU`, and the query nodes use a Prometheus data source named `prometheus`.

**2. Connect Hetzner Cloud** - [Hetzner](integration:hetzner)
The [Create Server](node:create-server) and [Delete Newest Node](node:delete-server) nodes manage worker servers. The template currently uses `ubuntu-24.04`, server type `cpx11`, location `fsn1`, and a Hetzner SSH key named `cluster-deploy`.

**3. Connect Slack** - [Slack](integration:slack)
Scale-up, scale-down, capacity, and bootstrap failure notifications go to `#cluster-ops` by default.

**4. Add the SSH private key secret**
The [Bootstrap Worker](node:join-ssh) node connects as `root` using a secret named `cluster-deploy` with key `private-key`. The matching public key must be uploaded to Hetzner as `cluster-deploy`.

**5. Review the autoscaling policy and workload**
The template scales up while [Below Max (4)?](node:check-max) is true and scales down while [Above Min (2)?](node:check-min) is true. The default workload is `nginxdemos/hello:latest`; update the bootstrap commands for your real worker process.

---

Let's start with the environment-specific values:

:::survey
What Slack channel should autoscaling notifications use? (e.g. `#cluster-ops`)
- [input]

Does the secret `cluster-deploy` already exist in your org?
- I need to create it
- It already exists

Should we keep the default Hetzner server settings (`cpx11`, `fsn1`, `ubuntu-24.04`)?
- Keep the defaults
- I need different settings
:::

Once you've shared those details, I'll update the canvas nodes and get the autoscaler ready for your cluster.
